name: Rodion Romanovich Raskolnikov
primary_sources: Crime and Punishment by Fyodor Dostoyevsky

distinctive_features: intelligent eyes with a troubled expression
visual_attributes.texture: rough, due to worn and old clothing
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: average height for a young adult male
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: often seen in a hunched posture due to stress and poverty

functional_attributes.primary_function: protagonist of a moral and psychological exploration

domain_specific_functional.narrative_role: explores themes of guilt, redemption, and morality
domain_specific_functional.psychological_conflict: internal struggle with pride and morality

relationships.description: Raskolnikov is devoted to his sister, Dunya.
Raskolnikov is devoted to his mother.
Raskolnikov accidentally kills her as a witness.
Raskolnikov confesses his crime to Sonia.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: explores the psychological and moral dilemmas of crime and punishment
primary_sources: Crime and Punishment by Fyodor Dostoyevsky

contrastive_constraints: NOT a character without moral conflict
